latest.title: Strategic Investment in Sodales AI
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6023L
GSTechnologies Ltd
09 July 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
9 July 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST", "GSTechnologies", the "Company" or the "Group")
Strategic Investment in Sodales AI
Investment supports GST's strategy to integrate artificial intelligence with its fintech, blockchain and digital payments platform
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce that it has entered into an investment agreement with Wide Ascent Limited ("WAL") in relation to a proposed strategic investment in Sodales AI Pte. Ltd. ("Sodales AI"), a Singapore-based artificial intelligence company established to develop and commercialise an enterprise-scale Agentic Artificial Intelligence Operating System ("Agentic OS").
Subject to the execution of definitive legal documentation and the satisfaction of customary completion conditions, GST has agreed to invest US$1.0 million in Sodales AI in exchange for a 10% equity interest ("Investment").
The investment will be funded from the Company's existing financial resources, including the recently announced US$10 million unsecured loan facility.
Highlights
·
Proposed investment of US$1.0 million for a 10% equity interest in Sodales AI.
·
Investment supports GST's strategy of combining artificial intelligence ("AI") with its existing fintech, blockchain and digital payments capabilities.
·
WAL to contribute its underlying Agentic AI technology platform and associated intellectual property through assignment or exclusive licence to Sodales AI.
·
The collaboration will support the development of an enterprise-grade Agentic AI neobanking platform for GST and its subsidiary, Angra Global Limited ("Angra").
·
The Investment, which is subject to the execution of definitive legal documentation and customary completion conditions, will be funded from the Company's existing financial resources, including its recently announced US$10 million unsecured loan facility.
Strategic Investment
The GST Board believes that artificial intelligence will become an increasingly important component of future financial services, complementing developments in digital payments, blockchain infrastructure and digital assets.
GST has continued to develop its technology platform across regulated payment services, blockchain infrastructure, stable token technology and cross-border payment solutions. The proposed Investment in Sodales AI represents a further step in that strategy as it is expected to provide the Group with exposure to enterprise-scale agentic AI technologies and the opportunity to collaborate on the development of AI-powered banking and payment solutions.
Under the investment agreement, Sodales AI will assist with the design, development and deployment of an agentic AI neobanking platform for GST and its subsidiary, Angra. The platform is intended to integrate autonomous AI agents with the Group's payment infrastructure, blockchain technology, stable token ecosystem and intelligent compliance processes, supporting the continued evolution of GST's digital financial services platform.
The Board believes that the convergence of artificial intelligence, blockchain technology and regulated financial services presents significant long-term opportunities and that this investment complements GST's objective of building an integrated technology platform for the next-generation of financial services.
About Sodales AI
Sodales AI is being established in Singapore as the principal operating company responsible for the ownership, development and commercialisation of an enterprise-scale Agentic OS.
WAL has substantially completed the first phase of development which comprises a number of specialist AI applications focused on executive productivity, enterprise research, education and social media automation. Under the proposed arrangements, WAL's intellectual property will be assigned or exclusively licensed to Sodales AI, forming the foundation of its technology platform.
Following completion of GST's proposed investment, Sodales AI intends to undertake a Series A funding round approximately six months later, targeting gross proceeds of between US$5 million and US$10 million for a 10% equity interest in Sodales AI, to accelerate product development, commercial deployment and international expansion. The valuation and investment terms will be determined at that time.
Further information on Sodales AI is available at
www.sodales.ai
⁠
.
Jack Bai, Chief Executive Officer of GS Technologies, commented:
"Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping financial services, and we believe it will become an increasingly important component of modern payment and banking platforms.
"We believe that our investment in Sodales AI complements the technological capabilities we have been building across payments, blockchain infrastructure and digital assets, while providing the opportunity to participate in the development of enterprise-scale Agentic AI.
"We are particularly excited by the opportunity to work with Sodales AI on AI-enabled banking solutions for the Group. This investment will be another important milestone in the execution of our strategy to build an integrated financial technology platform that combines artificial intelligence, blockchain technology and digital payments to create long-term value for our customers and shareholders."
Further Information
Completion of the proposed Investment in Sodales AI remains subject to the execution of definitive legal documentation and customary completion conditions. The Company will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5565I
GSTechnologies Ltd
17 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
17 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST", "GSTechnologies" or the "Company")
US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce that it has entered into a US$10 million unsecured term loan facility agreement with Clarivan Group Kommanditbolag, a company incorporated in Sweden (the "Lender") (the "Facility").
The Company intends to utilise the Facility to provide additional working capital and financial flexibility as it progresses its strategic growth initiatives, including for potential strategic acquisitions.
Tone Goh, Chairman of GSTechnologies, commented:
"The Facility provides GST with access to significant additional capital on attractive terms, enhancing our financial flexibility as we continue to execute our growth strategy. In particular, the Facility strengthens our ability to pursue strategic acquisition opportunities while supporting the ongoing development of our existing businesses and initiatives."
Key Terms of the Facility
·
Unsecured term loan facility of up to US$10 million.
·
The Facility is available in two tranches: US$5 million from 1 July to 31 July 2026; and US$5 million from 1 August to 30 August 2026.
·
Interest accrues on amounts drawn under the Facility at a rate of 5 per cent. per annum and is payable monthly in arrears.
·
The proceeds of the Facility may be used for general working capital purposes and potential corporate opportunities, including strategic acquisitions.
·
The Facility has a maturity date of 31 July 2030.
·
The Facility is repayable in full on 31 July 2030 unless prepaid, redeemed or converted in accordance with its terms.
·
Subject to the terms of the Facility agreement, the Lender may elect to receive repayment of the outstanding principal amount of the Facility together with accrued interest through the issue of a new class of non-voting preference shares to be created by the Company, with such shares carrying preferential dividend and liquidation rights.
·
Any conversion into preference shares would be based on the average ten-day volume weighted average price of the Company's ordinary shares immediately preceding the date of the conversion notice and would remain subject to applicable legal and regulatory requirements and any necessary shareholder approvals.
·
The Company may elect to prepay all or part of the Facility together with accrued interest in cash, ordinary shares and/or preference shares in the Company, subject to the terms of the Facility agreement.
·
The Company may elect to redeem the Facility in full prior to maturity, subject to payment of a redemption fee equal to 1 per cent. of the outstanding principal amount redeemed.
·
The Facility agreement contains customary representations, warranties, undertakings and events of default for an agreement of this nature, including restrictions on the incurrence of additional indebtedness.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3336G
GSTechnologies Ltd
01 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
1 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, provides an update to the announcement of 13 February 2026 regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
On 26 May 2026, hearings were held before the Assistant Registrar in the Singapore Supreme Court.
At these hearings, applications for a stay of proceedings numbered SUM 1210 (filed by the Semnet sellers) and SUM 1211 (filed by Chong Hoi Yan- a former Semnet employee), were dismissed in their entirety. A costs order of S$18,000 was awarded to Semnet.
A deadline to file a notice of appeal against the above decisions is set for 9 June 2026. Furthermore, a hearing will be held on 4 June 2026 for the Court to give the defence a direction for their case.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of GST shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Trading Services
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1673Y
GSTechnologies Ltd
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Asset Trading Services
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces the temporary suspension of the Company's crypto asset trading services with effect from 15 April 2026.
On 31 December 2025 the Company announced that GS Fintech UAB's crypto asset trading platform had transitioned to operate under the VASP-registered entity Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno") in Poland.
Whilst this can continue until 30 June 2026 the Company has determined that the Company's crypto asset trading activities should be aligned with the requirements of the EU Regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets ("MiCA") as soon as possible.
Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026, Finferno will temporarily suspend all services relating to the trading of crypto assets. The suspension will remain in place until a MiCA licence is obtained. Finferno's Terms of Service will be updated in connection with the suspension and will take effect on the same date.
Users of the platform are being informed and further announcements will be made in due course, as appropriate.

company_30s.what_they_do: GST Technologies Ltd builds software that lets businesses accept and manage digital payments, add AI‑powered analytics to their checkout flows, and run crypto‑based transactions. It sells a cloud‑based payment gateway, AI‑enhanced fraud detection modules and a small‑wallet platform for merchants.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Digital payments are growing fast; every retailer wants a single plug‑in that handles card, bank‑transfer and crypto. GST’s AI tools give merchants real‑time risk insights, cutting fraud costs and speeding up settlements, giving it a niche in the crowded fintech space.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been operating at a loss and has faced several regulatory stoppages, but it just secured a £5 million unsecured loan and a strategic AI investment. It also bought Metapay, a smaller payment‑processor, to broaden its product range. The business is still in a high‑cash‑burn phase, looking for a turnaround, and the shares are trading at a fraction of the company’s valuation.

rns_narrative: GST has pulled together a new capital package – a £5 million loan and a strategic AI investment – to keep the engine running.
It bought Metapay to add more payment‑processing capacity and to broaden its merchant base.
Meanwhile, regulators have temporarily halted its crypto‑trading arm and the company has been suspended a few times, signalling compliance headaches that could delay the recovery.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors are excited about the fresh funding, the AI partnership and the Metapay deal – all of which could turn the company into a more complete fintech platform.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics point to the debt load, the repeated suspensions and the fact that the company is still far from profitability, warning that the next regulatory blow could be fatal.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Early chatter was mostly hopeful after the loan announcement, but the recent suspension news has introduced caution, turning the mood into a cautious optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest is building up, which is a good sign that a surprise catalyst could create a big move.

near_term_catalysts: The drawdown of the £5 million loan – a cash injection that could fund operations for another 12‑18 months.
The official lifting of the crypto‑trading suspension – a clear sign regulators are comfortable with GST’s controls.
Completion of the Metapay integration – a new revenue stream that could push earnings into positive territory.
Any positive regulatory update or a new partnership announcement that demonstrates the company can meet compliance standards.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike on a breakout above the 52‑week low, a clean close above the 20‑day moving average, or a regulatory approval notice in the next 60 days.
expect_next.timing_regime: GST is still in the early phase of a recovery. The stock is likely still in capitulation, so a clear sign of regulatory clearance or a major funding announcement is needed before mean reversion can start.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If GST’s price rebounds to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 70‑100%. The deep crash means the market has already priced in the worst, so a bounce could be explosive. The new funding and AI partnership raise the probability that the company can finally break even.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory risk remains high – a second suspension could wipe out the loan and trigger a delisting. The debt load also means that if earnings stay negative, the company might default. Worst‑case, the shares could fall to a level where the company is forced to liquidate assets.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the amount you invest (the price could fall to near zero). Potential gain is 70‑100% if the stock recovers to the mid‑52‑week range, giving a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1:2.5.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average, size a position that uses no more than 10% of your discretionary account, and set a stop 5‑10% below that average to protect against a short‑term dip.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either the loan drawdown, a regulatory clearance, or a clear volume spike. Once any of those occur, re‑evaluate for a trade.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or files for bankruptcy, exit immediately; this is a terminal risk that cannot be recovered.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GST is a deep‑crashed fintech that just secured new funding and is fighting regulatory hurdles; a timely lift in compliance and the loan drawdown could trigger a strong mean‑reversion bounce with upside far exceeding the downside.
